title: Dorsaly Concord
content:
The Dorsaly Concord (1910) was a diplomatic agreement in which Ivan Drummond, peace delegate of Commonwealth of Tallmere, helped finalize terms at Valecross.

Implementation.

Commission reports from 1911 show gradual demobilization and the reopening of overland routes through the Valecross basin.

Negotiations.

Ivan Drummond, serving as peace delegate, chaired preliminary talks that began in spring 1909. Neutral observers from neighboring cantons attended the sessions.

Historical assessment.

Modern historians view the Dorsaly Concord as a stabilizing milestone, crediting Ivan Drummond's insistence on verifiable inspection clauses.

Reactions.

Newspapers in Commonwealth of Tallmere praised the compromise, while opposition leaders argued that naval access points near Valecross were not sufficiently protected.

Primary sources.

Archived minutes from the Valecross conference hall include Ivan Drummond's opening address and the ratification roll call.
